title: HALL RULES AND REGULATIONS
intro: In order to maintain decorum and conviviality in the Halls of Residence, the following rules and regulations shall apply;

subsections.subtitle: Eligibility for Hall Accommodation
subsections.content: Owing to the inadequacy of hall facilities, there is no guarantee that all registered students shall be accommodated.

sections.title: Allocation of bed space
sections.content: Allocation of bed space shall normally be done in the following order of preference:
list: i. Fresh students;
ii. Physically challenged students;
iii. Foreign students;
iv. Students on CGPA of 4.50 and above;
v. Active sportsmen and women;
vi. Final year student on the basis of first come, first served.
sections.note:
Returning students must obtain evidence of allotment of space from Student Affairs Division before payment of hostel fees.

All other students not within the categories listed above are advised to make arrangement for accommodation off campus where the halls have been filled. Students may seek information from the Student Affairs Officer who might have knowledge of available accommodation in nearby communities.

sections.title: Expectant Mothers
sections.content:
The University makes no provisions for child birth in the Hall of Residence. A pregnant student is therefore responsible for her private accommodation arrangement during the semester she expects to give birth. If she has been assigned a bed space for the part of the semester in which she expects to give birth and she moves out, the University will not be obliged to any rebate on the fees paid or payable for the part of the semester she stays out for the purpose of child birth.

All expectant mothers who have been offered accommodation in the Hall of Residence shall report to the Hall Wardens/Supervisors before taking up the accommodation. Expectant mothers are required to vacate the Hall six (6) weeks before the expected date of delivery. The University does not accommodate nursing mothers. The student is however, free to come back any time after delivery but NOT with the baby.

sections.title: Duration of Residency
sections.content: All Hall allocations will normally last for one academic year.

sections.title: Handling of Room Keys
sections.content: The last student to leave the room should drop the room key with the Porter on duty and sign out. The first student to return should sign in and obtain the room key.

sections.title: Absence from Hall
sections.content: No student may be away from Hall overnight without informing the Hall Porter. Students are required to sign the porter's log both on departure and on return.

sections.title: Visitors
list: a. Anybody who is not a registered resident of the Hall is a visitor.
b. Visitors are allowed into the room only between 4.00 pm and 8.00pm on week days and 10.00am to 9.00pm on Saturdays, Sundays and Public Holidays;
c. All visitors to students' rooms must do so through the Porter's Lodge where the visitors' book must be signed.

d. Female visitors are permitted in common rooms of male halls for purposes approved for hall functions. Similarly, male visitors are permitted in common rooms of female halls for purposes approved for hall functions.

sections.title: Common and Games Rooms
sections.content:
Responsibility for the use of Common Rooms and Games Rooms and their respective facilities and equipment shall lie with the Hall Supervisors.
Request for the use of Common Rooms and Games Rooms for functions, must be sought from the Hall Supervisors seventy-two (72) hours prior to the time the facility is needed.

sections.title: Others
list: a. Students must exercise great care and responsibility in the handling of all University equipment.

b. Loss or damage to any installation/equipment will be appropriately charged to the student(s) responsible. Where the culprit is unknown, the cost of the repairs/replacement may be assessed and charged by way of general levy on all students in the affected Hall.
c. A student who misuses any equipment/installation in the rooms shall be reported to the Hall Porter/House Keeper, Hall Officer/Warden for necessary action.
d. Students must exercise great caution and responsibility in the use of all University property in their rooms, toilet and other places in the Hall.
e. Furniture must neither be removed from the room nor exchanged between rooms.
f. Responsibility for the upkeep, custody of Hall property and maintenance of order in any room rests on the student(s) to whom the room is assigned.

g. Students are allowed to bring radio, record players and tape recorders into their rooms. However, such equipment/appliances should be used with a high sense of responsibility. It is expected that all musical equipment (audio/video) shall not be played beyond ten (10.00pm) in the rooms.

h. Users of musical appliances are, however, urged to recognize that they MUST NOT operate their equipment in such a manner as to annoy or cause inconvenience to other members of the Halls at any time.
I. In the interest of the health of residents of the Hall, smoking in the Halls is strictly prohibited. Any resident violating this regulation shall forfeit his/her room allocation.

j. Candles are not allowed in students' rooms. When there is power outage only rechargeable lanterns may be used. Kerosene lantern (bush lamp), matches, lighters, are not allowed. Generally, great care must be exercised by users to avoid fire outbreak.
k. Faulty electrical appliances, which risk fire outbreak, must not be used in the Hall.
l. All incidents of fire should be swiftly reported to the appropriate authority and investigated.
m. Students are responsible for their personal laundry. No laundry activity must be done in the Halls of Residence, except in specified places.

n. A student who is ill should report to the University Medical Unit for attention. A student suffering from an illness diagnosed as contagious must be immediately quarantined. Under no circumstances will such a student return to the Hall without a medical certificate of fitness.
o. Cooking and all culinary activities are strictly disallowed in the Halls.

p. Students are strongly advised to keep their rooms securely locked and keys removed and deposited with the Porter any time they move out of their rooms. Students are also advised in their own interest, not to keep valuables and large sums of money in their rooms. The University authorities will accept no responsibility for any loss of student property.
q. Unauthorized swapping of bed spaces (intra-or inter-Hall) between students is strictly prohibited.
r. Sale and procurement or other trafficking in bed spaces by students are strictly prohibited. Vendors and clients do so at the risk of summary eviction and loss of money.
s. Students are free to practice their respective religions. However, students in the Halls must remember that religious observances and practices are essentially individual and personal.
t. The conduct of cultural, social and religious activities should be in such a manner that will not disrupt other activities in the University.

u. Any hall resident who, either by himself/herself or in collaboration with others (students or non-students), engages in religious activities deemed to disturb the peace of other residents shall be summarily evicted from the Hall.

v. Students are advised to use bulletin boards installed in the Halls with the greatest sense of responsibility. It is illegal to deface or otherwise remove notices placed on bulletin boards without authorization. Students posting or removing notices without proper authorization will be liable to disciplinary action.

sections.title: Use of Electrical Appliances
list: a. Electrical appliances belonging to the University shall be signed for and returned to the Porter's lodge after use. Appliances belonging to the University must be treated with care. The University shall charge the student for any damage.
b. Users of electrical appliances recognized by the University should clear with and obtain a clearance slip from the Maintenance Officer before registering with the Hall Supervisor.
c. Only 13 amps. 3 pin fuses and earth plugs must be used in all electrical appliances.
d. Electrical appliances should normally be connected to all sockets only and never to light fittings. This rule must be strictly adhered to.
e. No alteration to any electrical installation in rooms is permitted.

sections.title: The Following Items should not be used in Residential Rooms or the Halls.
list: a. Cooker
b. Hot plate
c. Coal pot (Non-electrical)
d. Amplifie
e. Loudspeaker
f. Water heater (boiling ring)

sections.title: The Following Items May be used in Residential Rooms in the Halls.
list: a. Pressing Iron
b. Small Fridge
c. Calculator
d. Fan
e. Multipurpose Appropriately Fused Socket.

sections.title: Penalties for Violation of Hall Regulations
sections.intro: A student who violates any of the rules and regulations governing hall residency will be liable to disciplinary action commensurate with the offence as stipulated below:
list: a. Any student who allows visitors to stay in the hall overnight without clearance from the Hall Warden shall be liable to suspension from the Hall for one semester.
b. Students who use electronics or musical instruments/equipment in such a manner as to constitute nuisance shall have such equipment confiscated.
c. Any student caught harbouring another student shall be ejected from the hall.
d. Any student involved in acts of arson shall be ejected from the hall and handed over to the University Student Disciplinary Committee and the police thereafter.
e. Every fire incident shall be swiftly investigated and students found culpable or liable shall be handed over to the Student Disciplinary Committee for appropriate action.

f. Any student caught stealing, pilfering or being in possession of item(s) not belonging to him/her, without genuine explanation, shall be handed over to the Student Disciplinary Committee. If found guilty of stealing, such a student shall be expelled from the University.
g. All cases of fighting shall be referred to the Student Disciplinary Committee.

h. Use of weapons, chemicals, explosives, incendiary devices, etc., shall attract ejection from the halls of residence and the matter shall be referred to the Student Disciplinary Committee and the police for prosecution.
i. Forgery, alteration, misuse or mutilation of documents, records, identity card etc, will lead to eviction from the halls of residence.

j. Rioting or willful damage of University property or any misbehaviour capable of causing a breach of the peace will lead to ejection from the hall for one Academic Session and the culprit referred to the Student Disciplinary Committee.
k. Violators of regulations guiding religious observations in the halls of residence shall face disciplinary action, which shall be determined by the Committee of Wardens.
l. Those found liable for the defacing of walls shall be made to repaint such defaced walls and referred to the Student Disciplinary Committee.
m. Any student caught harbouring suspects shall forfeit his/her bed space, and other disciplinary actions may be taken against such a student or students.
n. Littering of halls is an offence punishable by appropriate sanctions to be determined by the Hall Warden(s).
o. Any student found coercing or luring others to join secret cults would face appropriate disciplinary action.

sections.title: Damage to Hall Facilities
sections.content: Students on allocation of Hall room may receive and sign for the appropriate keys. Items of furniture e.g. beds, mattresses and wardrobes, are provided by the University and students are required to inspect their rooms in the company of the Porter and to report any defect in writing within 24 hours to the Head Porter. Any defect discovered after this period shall be deemed to be the responsibility of the occupants who shall be held financially liable. Permission to use any of the University facilities in the halls shall be obtained through the porter.

sections.title: Return of Keys and University Property
sections.content: At the end of the semester or upon the eviction from the hall, students are required to return immediately all keys and University properties in their possession to the Hall Warden. Students who fail to comply with this instruction shall forfeit hostel accommodation for the rest of his/her stay in the University. Defaulting final year students shall have their letters of notification of results withdrawn and their graduations delayed until they return the keys/properties.

sections.title: Use of Hostel Facilities
sections.content: Application to the Dean through the Hall Supervisor for approval for hosting an event in the Hall must be received at the Student Affairs Office not later than seventy-two (72) hours to the event.

sections.title: Miscellaneous information
list: a. If any equipment or property is lost or damaged while in a student's care, the student concerned will pay for the cost of the damaged item.
b. The Student Affairs and the Security Department shall investigate all incidents of fire.
c. Students are responsible for all items or furniture and other school property in their rooms.
d. Students causing electrical failure in a hall or part of a hall will be charged the cost of restoration of power and shall face further disciplinary action.
e. All fixtures, fittings and furnishing must not be tampered with or modified in any way whatsoever.
f. No indecent pictures, posters, postcards, cuttings etc shall be displayed on the walls, doors and /or windows of rooms in Halls of residence.
g. Students must leave the hall not later than 12 noon of the following day on which the University officially closes except with the permission of the University authorities.
h. All complaints relating to student cafeteria services shall be directed to the Dean, Student Affairs.

sections.title: Personal Property and Sanitation
list: a. The University does not accept responsibility for any loss of personal property in the halls of residence. Students are therefore advised not to bring valuable and expensive personal items into the Halls. Students shall not leave personal property in their rooms during vacation without a written permission from the Hall Supervisor.
b. Waste and sanitary pads should be placed in the dustbins (receptacles) provided in the halls and toilets. Disposing of sanitary pads directly into the toilets is prohibited.

sections.title: Enforcement of Discipline
sections.content: The Dean, Student Affairs shall be responsible for ensuring that law and order is maintained within the precincts of the halls. It shall be lawful for the authority to take such actions at all times as it may deem necessary or expedient to maintain peace, protect property, promote and protect the safety and welfare of students.

sections.title: Vacation of Residence
sections.content: Students are not allowed to remain in Halls of Residence during vacation. If for any reason an exception is made to this rule, a specified charge to be determined by Management shall be paid in advance, per day for a bed space. Payment shall be made to the Bursary and payment receipt presented to the Student Affairs Division for clearance. Authorization to stay may be obtained from the Dean, Student Affairs.

sections.title: Rebate
sections.content: No student shall be entitled to rebate of fees on account of late registration or in respect of any period during which the student is sent out of the hall either for non-payment of fees or on disciplinary grounds.

sections.title: General Regulations
list: a. All students allocated to halls of Residence shall submit, on registration, two passport photographs for the hall album.
b. Where a final year student shares a room with any junior student(s), the final year student shall have first choice in the occupation of a bed space and wardrobe.
c. A student who wishes to swap an accommodation with another student shall present a written permission from the Dean, Student Affairs.
d. Partitioning of rooms is strictly prohibited in the Hall of Residence.
e. The Hall staff shall search all bags/luggage being taken in or out of the Halls at any time.
f. There shall be no bathing in unauthorized places.
g. There shall be no parties in the rooms. Students wishing to hold a social event shall apply to the Hall Warden who may give permission for use of the Common Room.
h. No student shall molest or obstruct the Hall staff in the course of performing their duties. Any student who attacks a Hall staff shall face Disciplinary Committee.
I. The University reserves the right to carry out routine checks in halls of residence.

sections.title: Closing of Doors
list: a. All entrance doors to the Hall shall be closed between 10pm and 5.00am every day.
b. Students returning to their Halls after 10pm except in exceptional cases shall be required to sign the late returnee's register.
c. A student whose names appear on this register more than three times in one semester shall forfeit his/her accommodation.

sections.title: Absence from the Halls of Residence
list: a. Students expecting to be away from the Halls of Residence for a night or longer period shall inform the Hall Warden/Supervisor and obtain permission before leaving.
b. In an emergency, the student shall leave a written statement with the Porter on duty explaining the nature of the emergency and indicating destination and probable duration of absence.
c. Upon return, the student shall report to the Hall Supervisor.
d. Students shall not leave the University prior to the designated time for holidays, or before the official vacation dates as stated in the University Calendar or official memorandum.

sections.title: Monthly Inspection of Hostels
sections.content: There will be monthly inspection of hostels by the Student Affairs Division.

sections.title: Valuables and Personal Property
sections.content: Students are advised to open accounts with banks for the security of their money and other valuables. Students who keep large sum of money and other valuables in their rooms do so at their own risk. Personal property shall NOT be left in the rooms during vacation.

sections.title: Ill–Health
sections.content: All students must register in the University Health Centre. Students who are ill shall report to the University Health Centre. Students who are seriously ill and are unable to report shall send for the Porter on duty as soon as possible.

sections.title: Cleanliness and Sanitation
sections.content: The premises and surroundings of the Halls of residence must always be kept in the best sanitary conditions.

subsections.subtitle: i. Pets and Domestic Animals
subsections.content: Animals and pets are not allowed in and around the Hall of Residence.

subsections.subtitle: ii. Litter Baskets
subsections.content: Trash or waste(s) shall be placed in receptacles provided, and students are advised to make use of them.
